packageRules.description: Vendored-from tracking. Renovate opens a PR bumping the digest (the signal); the vendored-resync-claude workflow re-vendors the file with Claude if upstream actually changed it, and the vendored-resync check gates the merge. rebaseWhen=never so Renovate doesn't clobber Claude's re-vendor commits on the branch.
prBodyNotes: ⚠️ Renovate only bumped the `# vendored-from:` digest. If upstream changed a vendored file, the **vendored-resync-claude** workflow re-vendors it onto this branch automatically — review Claude's changes before merging. If upstream did not touch our files, this is just a digest bump and is safe to merge as-is.

customManagers.description: ESPHome external_components git refs annotated with a renovate comment

ESPHome github:// package includes pinned with @version and a renovate comment. Version tags only; commit-SHA pins are handled by the git-refs manager below (the currentValue pattern requires a non-hex char so a 40-char SHA does not match here).

Track a GitHub repo's main-branch commit digest via git-refs. Used for esphome external_components pinned to a commit SHA (repos with no releases/tags) and for vendored-from source tracking. A move on main produces a digest-bump PR (or a dependency-dashboard entry) signalling a re-check / re-sync.
